CASE-2026-00042: Applicant relocated from Birmingham to Manchester in December 2025, triggering a change-of-circumstances review. Proof of address was received on 2 February 2026, however an income statement has been outstanding since 15 January 2026 and now exceeds the 56-day escalation threshold defined in POL-BR-003. A previous claim under REF-55102 was closed in November 2025. Immediate escalation to team leader is required.
CASE-2026-00091: Application for an event licence for an annual community festival with a capacity of 500 attendees. All documentation was received and verified, and a premises inspection on 5 March 2026 passed with no concerns noted. The applicant has held a personal licence for four years without incident. The case has been passed to a senior caseworker and a decision is pending.
CASE-2026-00107: Compliance check triggered by a third-party referral. A site visit on 10 March 2026 identified two serious regulatory breaches: two staff members operating without required qualification certificates and a six-month record-keeping failure. The case was escalated to a senior officer on 11 March 2026. The legal team has been notified and enforcement options are under active consideration.
CASE-2026-00133: Review triggered by a change of employment from full-time to part-time (22 hours per week). All required evidence — including P45, new employer details, and an updated income statement — was received and verified on 19 February 2026. The applicant's income falls below the eligibility threshold, indicating an entitlement increase under current policy. A draft decision recommending an award adjustment is awaiting team leader sign-off.
CASE-2026-00158: Annual reassessment review opened on 28 February 2026. An evidence request for a medical certificate and current income statement was issued on 5 March 2026. No response has been received to date. The 28-day reminder deadline has not yet been reached and the case is progressing normally at this stage. No prior issues are flagged on this claim.
CASE-2026-00172: Premises licence renewal for an established venue with a clean five-year history. All documentation was verified promptly, no objections were received during the public consultation period, and the licence was renewed without conditions on 28 February 2026. No further action is required — case is closed.
CASE-2026-00199: Routine annual compliance check opened on 15 March 2026. Evidence request for course delivery records, learner outcomes data, and tutor qualification certificates was issued on 18 March 2026. The organisation was last inspected 18 months ago with no issues found. The 28-day response window is currently in progress and no concerns have been identified at this stage.
CASE-2026-00214: Review triggered by a change of address notification in November 2025. Evidence was requested on 17 November 2025. Despite two reminder letters — sent at day 28 and day 42 — evidence remained outstanding at 64 days, exceeding the 56-day escalation threshold in POL-BR-003. The case was escalated to the team leader on 20 January 2026. The applicant's telephone number on record appears to be incorrect, preventing direct contact. Awaiting team leader direction on next steps.
CASE-2026-00231: New application for a premises licence for an outdoor venue. All required documents were received on 10 March 2026 and a 28-day public consultation received two objections from local residents, both relating to potential noise impact on nearby properties. The applicant submitted an updated noise management plan in response. Per POL-LA-003, the case must be referred to a senior caseworker given two or more substantive objections. A decision is pending senior review.
CASE-2026-00248: Compliance check opened on 1 March 2026 following an anonymous whistleblower report alleging inadequate health and safety practices at two sites. A full documentation package was received on 19 March 2026 and is under review. Records appear largely compliant; however, a six-week gap in the incident log for autumn 2025 has been identified. The organisation has been asked to provide clarification on this gap before the assessment can be completed.
